user.title: Custom IR — pick manufacturer & emitter
user.description: {device_count} device(s) from {manufacturer_count} manufacturer(s) loaded. Pick the manufacturer first, then the IR proxy emitter that should send its commands. Both fields support type-ahead search. To add a custom device, drop a YAML or JSON file into `<config>/customir_devices/` and re-open this dialog.

data_description.manufacturer: Devices are grouped by manufacturer. The 'Other' bucket contains entries without a manufacturer set (typically user-supplied YAML).
data_description.infrared_entity_id: Any entity from the core 'infrared' integration (e.g. an ESPHome IR Mate, a Broadlink RM hub).

device.title: Custom IR — pick device model
device.description: Pick the {manufacturer} device to control. {device_count} model(s) available. Each entry shows how many commands the device exposes.

data_description.device_key: After submission one button entity per command, plus one remote entity, will be created.

abort.no_emitters: No IR proxy emitters are loaded. Set up the core 'Infrared' integration plus an emitter (e.g. an ESPHome IR blaster) first.
abort.no_devices: No devices found in the catalog. Add device files under <config>/customir_devices/ or install the bundled catalog.
abort.no_devices_for_manufacturer: No devices for the selected manufacturer — restart the wizard.
abort.unknown_device: The selected device is no longer in the catalog. Try again.
abort.already_configured: This device is already configured for that IR proxy.

init.title: Custom IR — options
init.description: Compatibility settings for older IR emitter integrations.

data_description.legacy_timings: Enable only if you see ‘'int' object has no attribute 'high_us'’ when pressing a button. This emits Timing(high_us=…, low_us=…) objects instead of the modern signed-µs ints — a workaround for emitter integrations still on infrared-protocols < 2.0.0. The proper fix is to update your emitter.
